---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "deseq2"
namespace: "Differential expression"
description: "Performs differential expression analysis using DESeq2 on bulk samples or pseudobulk samples aggregated from single-cell data."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `deseq2`  
Namespace: `differential_expression`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/differential_expression/deseq2)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/differential_expression/deseq2)

:::

Note that this component only considers factors as explanatory variables, and excludes covariates from the analysis

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/differential_expression/deseq2/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/differential_expression/deseq2).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/differential_expression/deseq2/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).

## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name               |Description                                                                                                                                                                  |Attributes                         |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`          |Input h5mu file containing (pseudo-)bulk transcriptomic samples.                                                                                                             |`file`, required                   |
|`--modality`       |Which modality from the input MuData file to process.                                                                                                                        |`string`, default: `"rna"`         |
|`--input_layer`    |Input layer to use. If None, X is used. This layer must contain raw counts.                                                                                                  |`string`                           |
|`--var_gene_names` |Name of the .var field that contains gene symbols. If not provided, .var.index will be used.                                                                                 |`string`, example: `"gene_symbol"` |
|`--obs_cell_group` |.obs field containing the cell group information, for example per cell type or per cell cluster. If true, performs per-cell-group analysis with cell-group-specific results. |`string`                           |


### Arguments

|Name                 |Description                                                                                                                                                                                           |Attributes                                                                 |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--design_formula`   |The formula should be a tilde (~) followed by the variables with plus signs between them.  The design formula is used to estimate the dispersions and to estimate the log2 fold changes of the model. |`string`, required, example: `"~ disease + treatment"`                     |
|`--contrast_column`  |Column in the metadata to use for the contrast. This column should contain the conditions to compare.                                                                                                 |`string`, required, example: `"treatment"`                                 |
|`--contrast_values`  |Values to compare in the contrast column. First value is the control group, following values are comparison groups.  Values must be present in the fields specified for the design formula.           |List of `string`, required, example: `"ctrl", "stim"`, multiple_sep: `";"` |
|`--p_adj_threshold`  |Adjusted p-value threshold for significance, corrected with the Benjamini and Hochberg method. Genes with adjusted p-values below this threshold will be considered significant.                      |`double`, default: `0.05`                                                  |
|`--log2fc_threshold` |Log2 fold change threshold for significance. Genes with absolute log2 fold change above this threshold will be considered significant.                                                                |`double`, default: `0`                                                     |


### Outputs

|Name              |Description                                                                                                                                                                                         |Attributes                             |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output_dir`    |Output directory for DESeq2 results.  If cell groups are defined (using `--obs_cell_group`), the output folder will contain one CSV per cell group, otherwise it will contain a single output file. |`file`, required                       |
|`--output_prefix` |Prefix for output CSV files.  If no cell groups are specified, the output file will be named "{prefix}.csv". If cell groups are specified, files will be named "{prefix}_{cell_group}.csv".         |`string`, default: `"deseq2_analysis"` |

## Authors


  * Jakub Majercik [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/jakubmajercik) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/jakubmajercik) (author)

  * Dorien Roosen [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/dorien-er) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dorien-roosen) (author)

  * Dries De Maeyer [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/ddemaeyer) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dries-de-maeyer-b46a814) (contributor)

  * Weiwei Schultz (contributor)
